title: Photos: Stunning views of Sail Boston 250
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Over 60 international tall ships and military vessels arrived in Boston for the six-day Sail Boston 250 event.
The Parade of Sail featured ships from over 20 countries, including India's INS Sudarshini and Chile's Esmeralda.
Thousands of visitors gathered to watch the USS Constitution lead the parade into Boston Harbor on Saturday.

title: Red Sox draft SS Jake Schaffner with No. 20 pick
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: The Boston Red Sox selected shortstop Jake Schaffner with the No. 20 overall pick in the 2026 MLB Draft.
Schaffner, ranked 75th by MLB.com, played for the University of North Carolina and slashed .356/.467/.552 in 68 games.
At UNC, Schaffner stole 26 bases, had eight triples, and committed three errors, showcasing speed and defensive reliability.

title: Sail Boston 2026: The Grand Parade of Sail is rooted in rich history and tradition in the Commonwealth
author: Laurie Fullerton
bullets: The Grand Parade of Sail 2026 was led by the USS Constitution, showcasing historic ships in Boston.
The event celebrated maritime history, featuring ships like the Bluenose Two from Nova Scotia and Schooner Adventure from Gloucester.
Spectators and participants highlighted the educational and community aspects of the parade, emphasizing tradition and connection.

title: Brayan Bello named midseason AL ‘Cy Yuk’ winner by The Athletic for poor first-half performance
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Brayan Bello of the Red Sox was named the midseason AL 'Cy Yuk' winner for poor pitching by The Athletic.
Bello posted a 10.35 ERA in eight starts before being demoted to Triple-A Worcester.
Despite struggles as a starter, Bello excelled in relief, achieving a 0.71 ERA with 22 strikeouts in 25.1 innings.

title: Red Sox face all too familiar question heading into MLB trade deadline: Buy or sell?
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Red Sox close in on wild-card spot amid seven-game winning streak as MLB trade deadline approaches.
Boston's management faces dilemma of buying talent or selling assets, a recurring issue in past seasons.
Craig Breslow's job potentially at stake as team evaluates trade strategies to improve roster competitiveness.

title: Review & Setlist: Shakira delivers high-energy nostalgia and new beginnings at TD Garden
author: Maddie Browning
bullets: Shakira performed at TD Garden, Boston, marking her return with the 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran World Tour' after a music hiatus.
The concert featured a mix of old hits and new songs from her Grammy-winning album 'Las Mujeres Ya No Lloran'.
Shakira highlighted Colombian roots and women empowerment, incorporating diverse dance styles and elaborate stage setups.

title: Healey announces tax credit awards to build almost 700 new homes in six Mass. cities
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: The Healey-Driscoll administration grants $18.6 million in tax credits to build 662 homes in six Massachusetts Gateway Cities.
Projects in Fall River, Lawrence, Lowell, Pittsfield, Taunton, and Worcester will convert historic buildings into residential apartments.
HDIP tax credits aim to alleviate housing shortages, with 52 projects creating over 3,400 homes in 16 Massachusetts cities.

title: FBI arrests Mass. man in armed bank robberies in NH, NJ
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Joseph Sawyer, 52, was arrested in Albany, New York, for two armed bank robberies in NH and NJ.
Sawyer allegedly stole $6,000 in Nashua, NH, and $12,000 in Boonton, NJ, while brandishing a handgun.
The arrest was part of Operation Summer Heat 2.0, involving FBI and multiple local law enforcement agencies.

title: Motorcyclist missing since July 4 found dead in NH
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Wesley Grondin, 41, was found dead near his motorcycle in Shelburne, NH after being missing since July 4.
A resident searching for Grondin discovered his body and motorcycle off Route 2, prompting police notification.
Preliminary investigation shows Grondin veered into the eastbound lane, hitting a post; no other vehicles involved.

title: Gov. Maura Healey declares July 11 ‘Noah Kahan Day’
author: Morgan Rousseau
bullets: Gov. Maura Healey declared July 11 'Noah Kahan Day' to honor the singer's contributions to music and mental health awareness.
Noah Kahan became the first artist to hold four consecutive sold-out concerts at Fenway Park, drawing over 150,000 fans.
Kahan's music is celebrated for reflecting New England culture; he is also known for mental health advocacy through the Busyhead Project.
